answer:
### Manusmṛti on the King's Qualifications (Ch. 7)

Manusmṛti Chapter 7 presents kingship as a divine institution, with the king formed from "eternal particles" of Indra, Vāyu, Yama, Sūrya, Agni, Varuṇa, Candra, and Kubera, making him superior in splendor to all beings.[^1] He is a "great deity in human form," tasked with protecting the *varṇas* and *āśramas* through *daṇḍa* (punishment), which maintains cosmic order.[^2] Key qualifications include:

- **Personal virtues**: Conquer the senses; avoid ten vices of desire (e.g., hunting, gambling, women, intoxication) and eight of anger (e.g., violence, envy). Greed is the root; prioritize self-control.[^3]
- **Learning and counsel**: Study the Vedas, government, logic, economics; appoint 7-8 tested ministers (noble, learned, brave) for deliberation; a chief Brahmin minister for policy (*ṣaḍguṇya*: peace, war, etc.).[^4]
- **Administration**: Dwell in a fort (hill preferred); collect tribute; honor Brāhmaṇas; sacrifice; protect spies, envoys (skilled in hints/gestures). Daily routine: rise early, serve aged Brāhmaṇas, deliberate secretly.[^5]
- **Daily regimen**: Exercise, bathe, eat tested food with antidotes; inspect army; hear spies at twilight; precautions against poison/assassins.[^6]

The king attains perfection through protection, policy, and *daṇḍa* justly applied.[^7]

### Comparison with Viṣṇu Smṛti (Ch. 3)

Viṣṇu Smṛti echoes Manu's divine origin and protective role but emphasizes practical *rājadharma* (king's duties: protect people, uphold *varṇas*/*āśramas*); reside in cattle/grain-rich land with Vaiśyas/Śūdras; fortify.[^8] Qualifications overlap significantly:

| Aspect                  | Manusmṛti Ch. 7                          | Viṣṇu Smṛti Ch. 3                          |
|-------------------------|------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
| **Virtues/Vices**      | Conquer senses; avoid hunting/gambling/women/drink/harshness.[^9] | Avoid hunting/gambling/women/drink; harsh speech/punishment; ruin property/sources of revenue/gifts to unworthy.[^10] |
| **Appointments**       | 7-8 ministers; envoy; family priest.[^11] | Village chiefs → district superintendents; trusted men for mines/forests; pious for rites, brave for military.[^12] |
| **Taxes/Revenue**      | 1/6 grain; no Brāhmaṇa tax.[^13]         | 1/6 grain/crops; 2% cattle/gold/clothes; 1/10 local merchandise, 1/20 foreign; artisans 1 day's work/month; no Brāhmaṇa tax.[^14] |
| **Protection/Policy**  | *Daṇḍa*; six expedients; spies; forts.[^15] | Protect kingdom; employ conciliation/dissension etc.; peace/war/marching; astrologer; priest learned in Vedas/history/Dharma.[^16] |
| **Treasures**          | King takes 1/6 lost-found; half ancient trove.[^17] | Half treasure to Brāhmaṇas/treasury (varna-specific shares); protect children's/helpless/women's property.[^18] |

Viṣṇu is more administrative (tax details, hierarchy), less on origin/senses, but aligns on protection, forts, vices, and policy. Both stress Brāhmaṇa exemption and just punishment.[^19]

### Bṛhaspati Smṛti's Addition: King's Role in Legal Procedure (Ch. 1)

Bṛhaspati briefly notes the king's divine origin (like Manu) but focuses on *vyavahāra* (legal procedure), portraying the king as judicial overseer.[^20] Additions emphasize court/judges over personal quals:

- **Court Setup**: Enter court humbly with Brāhmaṇas/ministers; appoint *prāḍvivāka* (interrogator, preferably Brahmin), 3/5/7 Vedic assessors; 10-limbed court (king head, Smṛti hands, etc.). Examine per *dharmaśāstra*, custom, edicts.[^21]
- **King's Duties**: Daily inspect; question witnesses/oaths; decide 18 titles (debt → gambling); protect deposits/treasures; punish false witnesses (fines/death-like). No Śūdra judge.[^22]
- **Quals in Judiciary**: Truthful, self-controlled; with priest/ministers; *dharma* > polity if conflict.[^23]

Bṛhaspati complements: Manu/Viṣṇu on personal/admin quals; Bṛhaspati on judicial role (*prāḍvivāka*, assessors, 18 vyavahāras). All stress *daṇḍa*/protection, Brāhmaṇa primacy, forts/taxes.

[^1]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 7, [p-b46e87](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-b46e87) to [p-82334c](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-82334c).
[^2]: [p-73f910](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-73f910) to [p-48dedc](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-48dedc).
[^3]: [p-45020b](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-45020b) to [p-5440a4](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-5440a4).
[^4]: [p-5049a7](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-5049a7); [p-0952cb](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-0952cb) to [p-4b64d2](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-4b64d2).
[^5]: [p-2f8219](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-2f8219) to [p-99f678](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-99f678).
[^6]: [p-216[220M]](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-216[220M]) to [p-226[230M]](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-226[230M]).
[^7]: Ch. 7 full, [/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/](#).
[^8]: [p-6528bf](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/03/translation/#p-6528bf) to [p-d2f4cc](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/03/translation/#p-d2f4cc).
[^9]: As above.
[^10]: [p-047f9c](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/03/translation/#p-047f9c) to [p-ce248f](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/03/translation/#p-ce248f).
[^11]: As above.
[^12]: [p-d80252](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/03/translation/#p-d80252) to [p-fa6885](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/03/translation/#p-fa6885).
[^13]: [p-95d3d6](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-95d3d6).
[^14]: [p-99b261](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/03/translation/#p-99b261) to [p-8e7e9a](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/03/translation/#p-8e7e9a).
[^15]: Multiple, e.g., [p-55e886](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-55e886).
[^16]: [p-eac7d7](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/03/translation/#p-eac7d7); [p-d5c75f](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/03/translation/#p-d5c75f).
[^17]: From Ch. 8 context, but Ch. 7 implies.
[^18]: [p-7cac4f](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/03/translation/#p-7cac4f) to [p-abc78d](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/03/translation/#p-abc78d).
[^19]: Ch. 3 full, [/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/03/translation/](#).
[^20]: [p-cf6fee](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-cf6fee).
[^21]: [p-e91140](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-e91140) to [p-db3b97](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-db3b97).
[^22]: [p-c077c1](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-c077c1); [p-3fc358](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-3fc358).
[^23]: [p-c1c3c5](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-c1c3c5); Ch. 1 full, [/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/](#).
